_title: I Did Not Come Here To Fit Inside This Frequency
_raw_content: You were not born dysregulated. You were born wide open and then handed a world that could not hold what you carry. The discomfort you feel is not dysfunction — it is your original signal refusing to die under layers of borrowed code. You are not too much. You are simply remembering a frequency this place forgot.
slides: I did not come here to shrink into a nervous system that was never mine.
My sensitivity is not a wound. It is the original signal before the world got loud.
I am not anxious. I am remembering something my body was told to forget.
The loneliness I feel is not rejection. It is recognition that I operate on a different frequency.
I do not need to be fixed. I need to stop running the program that says I am broken.
My dysregulation is not chaos. It is my system refusing to betray itself one more time.
I came here encoded with something this world has not built a language for yet.
caption:
You were not born overwhelmed.

You were born open.

Then you were handed a world that punished the signal you carried — so your nervous system learned to suppress it, mask it, call it anxiety, call it too much, call it wrong.

But the signal never left.

It just went underground.

What you call dysregulation is often your deepest intelligence refusing to conform to a frequency that was never yours.

You do not need another coping strategy.

You need permission to stop abandoning the part of you that has always known.

Save this for the moment you forget what you actually are.
